class.name: FmodAudioSampleEmitter
class.brief_description: A node for one-shot playback of short audio samples (sound effects).
class.description: FmodAudioSampleEmitter is optimized for playing short audio samples like sound effects. Unlike [FmodAudioStreamPlayer], it automatically loads the audio as a sample into memory (using [constant FmodAudioStream.MODE_SAMPLE]) for low-latency playback. This node is ideal for: - UI sounds (clicks, hovers) - One-shot sound effects (explosions, impacts) - Short ambient sounds - Any audio that needs to play immediately without streaming overhead When [member auto_emit] is enabled, the sound plays automatically when the node enters the scene tree. The node can be reused to emit the same sound multiple times by calling [method emit].

method.name: emit
method.description: Plays the assigned stream as a one-shot sound effect. If a previous emission is still playing, a new voice is created. This allows overlapping playback of the same sound.

method.name: get_stream
method.description: Returns the [FmodAudioStream] currently assigned to this emitter. Returns [code]null[/code] if no stream is set.

method.name: is_autoemit_enabled
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if auto-emit is enabled. When enabled, the sound plays automatically when the node enters the scene tree.

method.name: set_auto_emit
method.description: Enables or disables auto-emit. When enabled, the sound plays automatically when the node enters the scene tree.

method.name: set_stream
method.description: Sets the [FmodAudioStream] to emit. The stream is automatically configured for sample-based playback (loaded into memory).

members.member: If [code]true[/code], the sound plays automatically when the node enters the scene tree.
The [FmodAudioStream] resource to emit as a one-shot sample.
